engineIntelligence.h2: Renault Scenic Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Renault Scenic in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Renault Scenic K9K engine - diesel 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injector wear - Delphi injectors prone to failure after 80k miles, causing rough idle and misfires.
Turbo failure - VGT actuator sticking leads to loss of power and black smoke on acceleration.
Fuel pump failure - High-pressure pump wear causes hard starting and reduced performance.

cards.altText: Renault Scenic H5F engine - petrol 1.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain stretch - Tensioner failure causes chain rattle and potential catastrophic engine damage.
Oil consumption - Piston ring wear leads to increased oil usage and blue exhaust smoke.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,100-£2,800 (supply only) Estimated from comparable H5F units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Renault Scenic K4M engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Ignition coil failure - Coils degrade after 70k miles causing misfires and rough running.
Dephaser solenoid wear - Variable timing issues lead to poor performance and warning lights.

engineGuide.h2: Renault Scenic Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Renault Scenic across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Ubiquitous 1.5 dCi across Scenic II-IV; reliable when maintained but injector/turbo failures drive replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: Expression 1.5 dCi, Dynamique 1.5 dCi, Privilege 1.5 dCi, Signature 1.5 dCi, Grand Scenic variants
commonFailures: Delphi injector wear - rough idle, misfires after 80k miles
VGT turbo actuator sticking - loss of power, black smoke
High-pressure fuel pump wear - hard starting, reduced performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 656 Renault Scenic engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Updated Euro 5 K9K variant for Scenic III/IV Grand; improved emissions but shares core failure patterns.
compatibleVariants: Grand Scenic Dynamique 1.5 dCi, Grand Scenic Privilege 1.5 dCi, Grand Scenic Signature 1.5 dCi
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging - reduced power, limp mode
DPF regeneration issues - warning lights, restricted performance
Injector seal leaks - fuel dilution of engine oil
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 837 Renault Scenic engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel Hybrid Assist
entries.history: First hybrid-assist diesel in Scenic IV; 48V system adds torque but complicates replacement sourcing.
compatibleVariants: Dynamique Nav 1.5 dCi Hybrid Assist, Privilege 1.5 dCi Hybrid Assist, Signature Nav 1.5 dCi Hybrid Assist
commonFailures: Hybrid belt tensioner wear - rattling noise, reduced assist
48V battery degradation - loss of hybrid function
Standard K9K injector/turbo issues still apply
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K 500 Renault Scenic engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Torque-rich 1.9 dCi for early Scenic II; robust block but turbo/DPF issues common on high-mileage examples.
compatibleVariants: Expression 1.9 dCi, Dynamique 1.9 dCi, Privilege 1.9 dCi, Grand Scenic 1.9 dCi
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing failure - blue smoke, loss of boost
Swirl flap mechanism wear - inlet manifold damage risk
EGR cooler leaks - coolant loss, overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for F9Q 812 Renault Scenic engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Modern Euro 6 1.6 dCi replacing older 1.9/2.0 units; efficient but complex emissions systems increase repair costs.
compatibleVariants: Dynamique Nav 1.6 dCi, Signature Nav 1.6 dCi, Grand Scenic 1.6 dCi
commonFailures: AdBlue injector clogging - NOx warnings, limp mode
Turbo actuator electrical faults - intermittent power loss
Timing chain tensioner wear - rattle on cold start
entries.cta: Get quotes for R9M 414 Renault Scenic engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Powerful 2.0 dCi for performance-oriented Scenic III; sought-after but higher replacement costs reflect complexity.
compatibleVariants: Privilege 2.0 dCi, Signature 2.0 dCi, Grand Scenic 2.0 dCi
commonFailures: Dual-mass flywheel wear - clutch judder, rattling noise
High-pressure fuel pump failure - hard starting, power loss
Turbo wastegate sticking - over/under-boost faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for M9R 722 Renault Scenic engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Reliable naturally aspirated 1.6 16V powering early Scenic I/II; simple design aids affordability of replacements.
commonFailures: Ignition coil failure - misfires, rough running after 70k miles
Dephaser solenoid wear - variable timing faults, warning lights
Water pump leakage - coolant loss, overheating risk
entries.cta: Get quotes for K4M 761 Renault Scenic engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level 1.4 16V for budget Scenic buyers; adequate power but less common in replacement market.
commonFailures: Coil pack degradation - intermittent misfires, poor fuel economy
Throttle body carbon buildup - hesitation, unstable idle
Camshaft position sensor failure - starting difficulties
entries.cta: Get quotes for K4J 814 Renault Scenic engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Powerful 2.0 16V for RX4 and top-spec Scenic II; sought by enthusiasts but higher replacement costs reflect demand.
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner failure - belt slip if service intervals missed
Oil consumption from valve stem seals - blue smoke on deceleration
Crankshaft position sensor faults - stalling, no-start conditions
entries.cta: Get quotes for F4R 740 Renault Scenic engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L TCe Petrol Turbo
entries.history: Downsized turbo petrol for Scenic III/IV; timing chain tensioner issues dominate replacement enquiries for this engine.
compatibleVariants: Dynamique Nav 1.2 TCe, Signature 1.2 TCe, Signature Nav 1.2 TCe
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch/tensioner failure - catastrophic engine damage risk
Turbo oil feed coking - reduced boost, turbo failure
Carbon buildup on intake valves - power loss, rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for H5F 412 Renault Scenic engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L TCe Petrol Turbo
entries.history: Updated 1.3 TCe co-developed with Mercedes for Scenic IV; improved reliability but still early in replacement cycle.
compatibleVariants: Dynamique Nav 1.3 TCe, Signature Nav 1.3 TCe, Grand Scenic 1.3 TCe
commonFailures: Camshaft cover oil leak - burning oil smell, potential fire risk
High-pressure fuel pump wear - hard starting, reduced performance
Turbo wastegate actuator faults - inconsistent boost pressure
entries.cta: Get quotes for H5H 470 Renault Scenic engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Renault Scenic Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault Scenic's most common engine issues centre on the 1.5 dCi K9K diesel and 1.2 TCe H5F petrol families, which dominate UK replacement demand. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Scenic.

problems.h4: 1.2 TCe Timing Chain Failure - Why H5F Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Renault Scenic III/IV 1.2 TCe (2012-2019, H5F 408, H5F 412)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure allowing chain stretch; metal debris from worn guides accelerates damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents major damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Repair valve damage from chain jump; replace affected components
repairOptions.longevity: Only if block undamaged; high risk of recurrence

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution for high-mileage Scenic

problems.recommendation: On a Scenic III/IV worth £2,000-£5,000, a preventative chain kit makes sense if caught early. Once rattling is audible or performance drops, replacement with a rebuilt H5F including the updated tensioner design is the only cost-effective route.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt H5F engine prices for your Renault Scenic

problems.h4: 1.5 dCi Injector Failure - K9K Fuel System Issues
problems.affectedModels: Renault Scenic II/III/IV 1.5 dCi (2003-2022, K9K 656, K9K 837, K9K 500)
problems.rootCause: Delphi injector seals degrade allowing fuel leak into oil; high-pressure pump wear reduces injection precision.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace injector seals, flush oil system, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term fix; addresses symptom not root cause

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors, code to ECU, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy; 40-60k mile expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K9K with upgraded injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Scenic; eliminates repeat failures

problems.recommendation: For Scenics under 100k miles with otherwise good service history, injector replacement offers good value. On higher-mileage examples or where oil dilution has occurred, a rebuilt K9K with updated components is the safer long-term investment.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K9K Renault Scenic engine replacement

problems.h4: 1.5 dCi Turbo Failure - Loss of Power & Black Smoke
problems.affectedModels: Renault Scenic II/III 1.5 dCi (2003-2016, K9K variants)
problems.rootCause: VGT actuator sticks from carbon buildup; oil starvation from clogged feed lines damages turbine bearings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean/replace actuator, reset adaptations, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Only if turbine intact; temporary fix for sticking issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit refurbished turbo, new gaskets, oil flush
repairOptions.longevity: Good option if engine block healthy; 50-70k mile expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded turbo, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable for high-mileage Scenic; addresses root causes

problems.recommendation: If turbo failure is isolated and the engine has good compression, a quality refurbished turbo offers sensible value. Where oil contamination or bearing damage is suspected, a rebuilt K9K eliminates the risk of repeat failure.
problems.cta: Compare K9K turbo replacement costs for your Renault Scenic

problems.h4: 1.6 Petrol Head Gasket Failure - K4M Overheating Issues
problems.rootCause: Coolant passage corrosion combined with thermal cycling weakens gasket; overheating accelerates failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace gasket, skim head if needed, refill coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block undamaged; requires precise torque procedure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Recondition head, replace valves/guides, pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Good option if block compression healthy; extends life

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K4M with upgraded gasket, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for older Scenic; eliminates repeat overheating risk

problems.recommendation: On a well-maintained Scenic I/II under £1,500 value, head gasket repair can extend life cost-effectively. For higher-value examples or where block damage is suspected, a rebuilt K4M offers greater peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K4M Renault Scenic engine replacement

problems.h4: DPF Blocked & EGR Faults - Diesel Emissions System Issues
problems.affectedModels: Renault Scenic III/IV 1.5/1.6 dCi (2009-2022, K9K, R9M)
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents DPF regeneration; EGR valve clogs from soot, triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-car DPF clean, EGR valve clean, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; requires motorway driving post-service

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace DPF and/or EGR valve, code to ECU, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy; 40-60k mile expectancy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with emissions components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage Scenic; eliminates repeat emissions faults

problems.recommendation: For Scenics used primarily on short trips, DPF/EGR service plus driving habit changes can restore function. On high-mileage examples where emissions faults recur, a rebuilt diesel engine with updated components offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt diesel engine prices for your Renault Scenic

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Renault Scenic
finalCta.paragraph: The Renault Scenic's most common engine failures - timing chain stretch on 1.2 TCe, injector/turbo issues on 1.5 dCi, and emissions system faults on later diesels - don't have to mean scrapping your family MPV. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Renault Scenic specialists for rebuilt, reconditioned and used engines. Every unit includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Renault Scenic Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Renault Scenic and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Renault Scenic Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Renault Scenic in the UK is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Renault Scenic 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L K9K diesel is the backbone of the Scenic range, appearing across Scenic II, III and IV in multiple power outputs. Its compact size and strong torque made it the default choice for UK families, though injector and turbo failures drive significant replacement demand.
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Scenic 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Renault Scenic 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced on Scenic III facelift and standard on Scenic IV, the 1.6L R9M diesel replaced older 1.9/2.0 units with improved emissions compliance. Its complex AdBlue/DPF systems increase repair costs but offer better fuel economy for high-mileage owners.
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Scenic 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Renault Scenic 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L F9Q diesel powered early Scenic II models with robust torque for towing and motorway cruising. While mechanically simple compared to later units, turbo and swirl flap issues are common on high-mileage examples.
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Scenic 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Renault Scenic 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L M9R diesel offered flagship performance for Scenic III, appealing to buyers needing strong towing capacity. Its dual-mass flywheel and complex turbo system increase replacement costs but deliver refined power.
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Scenic 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Renault Scenic 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.4L K4J petrol powered base-spec Scenic I/II models. Its simple naturally aspirated design aids affordability but lower power output limits appeal for heavier Grand Scenic variants.
items.cta: Compare Renault Scenic 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Scenic 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The versatile 1.6L K4M petrol balanced adequate power with reasonable fuel economy across Scenic I and II. Its widespread use means good parts availability and competitive replacement pricing for budget-conscious owners.
items.cta: Compare Renault Scenic 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Scenic 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L F4R petrol delivered strong performance for RX4 and top-spec Scenic II models. Enthusiast demand keeps replacement values higher, but its robust design aids longevity when properly maintained.
commonFailurePoints: oil consumption from valve stem seals
items.cta: Compare Renault Scenic 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Scenic 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The downsized 1.2L H5F TCe turbo petrol aimed to combine efficiency with adequate power for Scenic III/IV. However, timing chain tensioner failures dominate replacement enquiries for this engine family.
items.cta: Compare Renault Scenic 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Scenic 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The co-developed 1.3L H5H TCe turbo petrol replaced the 1.2L unit on Scenic IV with improved refinement and reliability. Early replacement data suggests fewer catastrophic failures but cam cover oil leaks require attention.
items.cta: Compare Renault Scenic 1.3L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Renault Scenic needs? fuelTypes.h2: Renault Scenic Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Renault Scenic engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 1.4 16V petrol to the latest 1.5 dCi Hybrid Assist.

items.title: Renault Scenic Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel power dominates the Scenic range across all four generations, with the 1.5L K9K family accounting for the majority of UK replacement enquiries due to its ubiquity and well-documented failure patterns.
items.descriptor: Diesel power dominates the Scenic range across all four generations, with the 1.5L K9K family accounting for the majority of UK replacement enquiries due to its ubiquity and well-documented failure patterns.
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Scenic Diesel Engine Families
families: K9K - 1.5L dCi across Scenic II/III/IV; injector/turbo failures drive high replacement volume
F9Q - 1.9L dCi for early Scenic II; robust but turbo/DPF issues on high mileage
R9M - 1.6L dCi for Scenic III/IV; complex emissions systems increase repair costs
M9R - 2.0L dCi for performance Scenic III; sought-after but higher replacement pricing
foundIn: Scenic IV (2016-2022) - K9K 110hp Hybrid Assist, R9M 130hp
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and towing capability
K9K injector wear after 80k miles causing rough idle and misfires
Turbo actuator sticking leading to loss of power and black smoke
High UK replacement demand on Dynamique and Privilege 1.5 dCi variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Scenic Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Renault Scenic Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Scenics appeal to lower-mileage and urban drivers, with the simple K4M 1.6L offering affordable replacements while the turbocharged H5F/H5H TCe units require specialist attention for timing chain and oil leak issues.
items.descriptor: Petrol Scenics appeal to lower-mileage and urban drivers, with the simple K4M 1.6L offering affordable replacements while the turbocharged H5F/H5H TCe units require specialist attention for timing chain and oil leak issues.
items.familiesLabel: Common Renault Scenic Petrol Engine Families
families: K4M - 1.6L 16V naturally aspirated; reliable and affordable to replace
K4J - 1.4L 16V entry-level; adequate power for lighter Scenic I/II variants
F4R - 2.0L 16V for RX4 and top-spec; enthusiast demand keeps values higher
H5F/H5H - 1.2/1.3L TCe turbo; timing chain tensioner failures dominate enquiries
foundIn: Scenic III/IV (2009-2022) - H5F 1.2L TCe, H5H 1.3L TCe
knownFor: Lower purchase price and simpler maintenance than diesel equivalents
H5F timing chain stretch causing catastrophic damage if not addressed early
K4M ignition coil failures leading to misfires after 70k miles
F4R robust design but higher fuel consumption for heavier Grand Scenic
items.cta: Compare Renault Scenic Petrol engine prices

items.title: Renault Scenic Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: The 1.5 dCi Hybrid Assist system on Scenic IV adds 48V electric torque assistance for improved low-speed efficiency, though replacement requires specialists familiar with the hybrid belt drive system.
items.descriptor: The 1.5 dCi Hybrid Assist system on Scenic IV adds 48V electric torque assistance for improved low-speed efficiency, though replacement requires specialists familiar with the hybrid belt drive system.
families: K9K 500 - 1.5L dCi with 48V Hybrid Assist (Scenic IV Dynamique/Signature Nav)
48‑volt starter‑generator recovers braking energy to recharge belt‑driven battery
Electric torque assistance under acceleration reduces diesel load and fuel consumption
Engine replacement requires hybrid system inspection and belt tensioner verification
importantNotes: No plug‑in hybrid or full electric Scenic variant was sold in the UK before 2024
MHEV engine replacement requires hybrid system inspection by qualified technician
Availability is more limited than standard K9K engines; allow extra lead time

engineYears.h2: Renault Scenic Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Renault Scenic's UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Scenic I Facelift: K4M Petrol & F9Q Diesel Arrive
years.description: The facelifted Scenic I introduced 16-valve petrol engines and the first common-rail F9Q diesel, setting the template for future Scenic powertrains.
keyChanges: Introduction of 16V petrol engines (K4J 1.4, K4M 1.6, F4R 2.0)
First common-rail F9Q 1.9 dCi diesel with improved refinement
RX4 4x4 variant launched with F4R petrol and F9Q diesel options
knownFor: Simple, robust engines with good parts availability
Timing belt changes critical at 72k miles to avoid catastrophic failure
RX4 4x4 system prone to transfer box issues on high-mileage examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2003 Renault Scenic replacement engines
years.ticker: Scenic I 1.6 engine replacement • Scenic RX4 2.0 engine • F9Q diesel for Scenic 2002

years.description: The all-new Scenic II established the K9K 1.5 dCi as the UK's default family MPV engine, with Grand Scenic seven-seater variant expanding the range.
keyChanges: Introduction of K9K 1.5 dCi in 85hp and 105hp outputs
Grand Scenic seven-seater variant launched with longer wheelbase
Facelift in 2006 added updated engines and interior revisions
knownFor: K9K injector wear after 80k miles causing rough idle and misfires
Turbo actuator sticking on high-mileage examples leading to limp mode
Grand Scenic's extra weight increases strain on same engine families
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2009 Renault Scenic replacement engines
years.ticker: Scenic II 1.5 dCi engine • K9K 832 replacement • Grand Scenic engine swap

years.preview: Scenic III: H5F TCe Timing Chain Issues Emerge
years.description: Scenic III introduced downsized turbo petrol (H5F 1.2 TCe) alongside refined K9K diesel, but timing chain failures on the TCe drive significant replacement demand.
keyChanges: Introduction of H5F 1.2L TCe turbo petrol with direct injection
Updated K9K 1.5 dCi with Euro 5 emissions compliance
R9M 1.6 dCi introduced on facelift models replacing older 1.9/2.0 diesels
knownFor: H5F timing chain tensioner failure causing catastrophic engine damage
K9K injector and turbo failures remain common on high-mileage examples
R9M complex emissions systems increase repair costs but improve economy
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2016 Renault Scenic replacement engines
years.ticker: Scenic III 1.2 TCe timing chain • K9K 656 injector replacement • R9M 1.6 dCi engine

years.preview: Scenic IV: Hybrid Assist & Updated TCe
years.description: The final combustion Scenic introduced 48V Hybrid Assist on the K9K diesel and updated H5H 1.3L TCe petrol, with improved refinement but continued complexity in emissions systems.
keyChanges: K9K 500 1.5 dCi Hybrid Assist with 48V belt-driven starter-generator
H5H 1.3L TCe co-developed with Mercedes replacing H5F 1.2L unit
R9M 1.6 dCi standardised across diesel range with Euro 6 compliance
popularModels: Renault Dynamique Nav 1.5 dCi Hybrid Assist
knownFor: Hybrid Assist belt tensioner wear causing rattling and reduced efficiency
H5H cam cover oil leaks requiring early attention to prevent fire risk
R9M AdBlue/DPF systems prone to clogging on short-journey usage
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2022 Renault Scenic replacement engines
years.ticker: Scenic IV Hybrid Assist engine • H5H 1.3 TCe oil leak • R9M AdBlue system fault

faq.h2: Renault Scenic Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Renault Scenic engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Renault Scenic engine typically costs £650-£2,800 supply-only depending on engine code and variant. The ubiquitous 1.5 dCi K9K is at the lower end, while turbo petrol units like the 1.2 TCe command higher prices due to complexity and lower availability.
keyPoints: K9K 1.5 dCi rebuilt: £650-£2,100 supply-only
H5F 1.2 TCe rebuilt: £1,100-£2,800 supply-only
Supply & fit adds £450-£850 labour depending on garage
Prices vary by engine code, mileage and supplier location
items.cta: Compare Renault Scenic engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Renault Scenic engine?
items.answer: For Scenic II/III models valued under £1,500, engine replacement rarely makes financial sense unless you plan long-term ownership. For Scenic IV or well-maintained examples worth £3,000+, a rebuilt engine with warranty can extend life cost-effectively.
keyPoints: Scenic II (2003-2009) values: £500-£1,500 typically
Scenic IV (2016-2022) values: £3,000-£8,000 typically
Rebuilt K9K engine cost: £650-£2,100 plus fitting
12-month warranty on rebuilt units protects your investment
items.cta: Get a tailored quote for your Renault Scenic engine replacement

items.question: Why do Renault Scenic 1.2 TCe engines fail?
items.answer: The 1.2 TCe H5F engine suffers from timing chain tensioner failure allowing chain stretch, which can cause catastrophic valve damage. Oil starvation from clogged turbo feed lines also accelerates wear on high-mileage examples.
keyPoints: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure after 60-90k miles
Chain stretch causes rattling noise then valve contact
Turbo oil feed coking reduces lubrication to bearings
Updated tensioner design in rebuilt units addresses root cause
items.cta: Compare rebuilt H5F engine prices for your Renault Scenic

items.question: What is the most reliable Renault Scenic engine?
items.answer: The naturally aspirated K4M 1.6L petrol is widely regarded as the most reliable Scenic engine due to its simple design and proven longevity. For diesel, well-maintained K9K 1.5 dCi units with full service history can also deliver strong reliability.
keyPoints: K4M 1.6L petrol: simple design, affordable parts, 150k+ mile potential
K9K 1.5 dCi: reliable with regular oil changes and injector checks
Avoid high-mileage H5F 1.2 TCe without timing chain service
Full service history is critical for any Scenic engine longevity
items.cta: Find reliable rebuilt engines for your Renault Scenic variant

items.question: Can I fit a Grand Scenic engine to a standard Scenic?
items.answer: Engines are generally interchangeable between standard and Grand Scenic variants sharing the same engine code, but always verify ECU compatibility, wiring harness connections and emissions equipment before purchasing a replacement unit.
keyPoints: Same engine code (e.g., K9K 656) usually fits both variants
Grand Scenic's extra weight may require ECU recalibration
Check wiring harness and sensor compatibility before fitting
DVLA registration lookup confirms exact engine code for your car
items.cta: Verify engine compatibility for your Renault Scenic registration

items.question: How long does a Renault Scenic 1.5 dCi engine last?
items.answer: With regular servicing including oil changes every 12k miles and injector checks at 80k miles, a K9K 1.5 dCi can reach 150,000-200,000 miles. Neglecting oil quality or ignoring injector warning signs reduces lifespan significantly.
keyPoints: Full service history critical for K9K longevity
Oil changes every 12k miles maximum with correct specification
Injector seal checks recommended at 80k miles to prevent oil dilution
Turbo actuator maintenance prevents premature failure
items.cta: Get quotes for long-life rebuilt K9K engines for your Scenic

items.question: Do you fit Renault Scenic engines?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists offering supply & fit services for Renault Scenic engines. Labour costs typically add £450-£850 to the engine price depending on location and garage rates.
keyPoints: Supply & fit available nationwide through our specialist network
Labour costs vary by region: £450-£850 typical range
All fitting work covered by minimum 12-month warranty
Book supply & fit when requesting your engine quote
items.cta: Find Renault Scenic engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: What warranty comes with a Renault Scenic engine?
items.answer: Every rebuilt or reconditioned Renault Scenic engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for the replacement unit itself.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Warranty covers internal components, not external fittings
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
Confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: Compare warranty terms on Renault Scenic engine quotes

items.question: Are used Renault Scenic engines reliable?
items.answer: Used engines from low-mileage accident-damaged Scenics can offer good value for older models, but always request compression test results and service history. Rebuilt units with warranty provide greater peace of mind for daily drivers.
keyPoints: Used engines: £450-£1,600 but condition varies significantly
Always request compression/leak-down test report before purchase
Rebuilt units include new wear components and 12-month warranty
Best for Scenic I/II owners where car value is under £1,500
items.cta: Check used vs rebuilt engine options for your Renault Scenic

items.question: How do I find my Renault Scenic engine code?
items.answer: Your engine code is stamped on the engine block near the gearbox bellhousing, listed on your V5C registration document under "Engine number", and can be instantly verified by entering your registration on EngineMarket.
keyPoints: V5C document lists engine number/code under section D.2
Physical stamp location: engine block near gearbox bellhousing
Common codes: K9K (diesel), K4M (petrol), H5F (TCe)
DVLA lookup via registration confirms exact code instantly
items.cta: Enter your reg to identify your Renault Scenic engine code
